response: To determine the total number of writing prompts, we first calculate the number of groups. With 24 residents divided into groups of 6, there are 4 distinct groups. Emily allocates 3 initial prompts to each group, resulting in 12 prompts for the first round (4 groups multiplied by 3 prompts). She also has a set of 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among these same 4 groups. Adding the initial 12 prompts to the 10 extra ones gives a total of 22 writing prompts needed for the session. This collection of materials serves as a vital library of creative ideas for the residents. By organizing this library of prompts so effectively, Emily ensures that every participant has ample material to explore their thoughts. The END.
